div1.entryFree:
Beneath. 1) Adv., below: it droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven upon the place b. Merch. IV, 1, 186. that next (is disdained) by him b. Troil. I, 3, 131. hears it roar b. Hml. I, 4, 78. b. is all the fiends,' Lr. IV, 6, 129. Adjectively: this b. world, Tim. I, 1, 44 (cf. Under).
2) Prepos. lower than, below, under: b. the sky, Wint. I, 2, 180. b. the moon, Lr. IV, 6, 26. the dust b. thy foot, V, 3, 137 (Ff below). whose heads grow b. their shoulders, Oth. I, 3, 145. sinks b. the yoke, Mcb. IV, 3, 39. from below your duke to b. your constable, All's II, 2, 32. damned b. all depth in hell, Oth. V, 2, 137. so far b. your breeding, Tw. V, 331. flatter b. abhorring, Cor. I, 1, 172. it smites me b. the fall I have, Ant. V, 2, 172. not b. him in fortunes, Cymb. IV, 1, 11.
